_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch, Richmond-area districts 78-82 (state tier). Betsy Carr (HD-78, D), Rae Cousins (HD-79, D), Destiny LeVere Bolling (HD-80, D), Delores McQuinn (HD-81, D), Kimberly Pope Adams (HD-82, D). Evidence-first finalization from VPAP caucus-unity + LIS/Richmond Sunlight roll-calls/co-patron lists + ReproRising 2025 scorecard + Giffords + Ballotpedia + campaign sites. STANDOUT: Pope Adams has a HARD pro-2A record (3 NAY votes vs gun-control bills HB1524/SB727/HB871) despite a Giffords endorsement, plus a pro-police-funding platform = real documented credit. Carr earns refuse_federal_overreach credit for a 'Defend the Guard' YES (99-0). DISCIPLINE: felon-voting-restoration amendment (HJ2) co-patronage does NOT map to the 5 election_integrity cells, so election_integrity left null and the fact recorded in notes; not forced into a FALSE.

profile.religion: Episcopalian; served 16 years as outreach director at St. Paul's Episcopal Church, downtown Richmond, and founded the faith-community/school Micah Initiative
profile.background: VA House of Delegates, HD-78 (Richmond/part of Chesterfield); in office since 2010 (old HD-69, redistricted to HD-78 in 2024). Treasurer of the House Democratic Caucus; committees include Appropriations, Transportation, General Laws, Rules. b. 1946; Hollins College; former Richmond School Board.

betsy-b-carr.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-78 (Richmond), sitting since 2010. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record where evidence exists. Life: voted YES on HJ1 (2025) reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; ReproRising 2025 = A+ 'Champion'; listed as a patron to expand later-term abortion and endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia (iVoterGuide). Marriage: voted FOR the marriage-equality constitutional amendment (HJ9 2025 / HJ3 2026) repealing the man-woman definition. 2A: Giffords-endorsed; sponsored high-capacity-magazine limits and firearm bans in school zones/libraries. ONE DOCUMENTED CROSS-PARTY CREDIT: voted YES on HB2193 'Defend the Guard' (2025), a 10th-Amendment/anti-commandeering state-sovereignty measure (passed 99-0, unanimous — weak but on the record). Co-patron of HJ2 (2026) automatic felon-voting-rights restoration (recorded here but not mapped to the election-integrity ballot-mechanics cells). Episcopalian; long faith-community service via the Micah Initiative did not produce any social-conservative voting deviation.

0.note: Voted YES on HJ1 (2025), the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; ReproRising 2025 grade A+ 'Champion' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Listed as a patron of legislation to expand later-term abortion; voted the pro-abortion-access slate — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia.

0.note: Voted in favor of the marriage-equality constitutional amendment (HJ9 2025 / HJ3 2026) repealing Virginia's one-man-one-woman marriage provision.

1.note: Voted YES to enshrine a right to same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

0.note: Voted YES on HB2193 'Defend the Guard' (2025), restricting federal deployment of the VA National Guard absent a Congressional war declaration — an anti-commandeering/state-sovereignty measure (passed 99-0, unanimous).

1.note: Giffords-endorsed; the endorsement cites her sponsoring high-capacity-magazine limits and firearm bans in school zones/libraries — opposite of the 2A position on magazine limits/bans.

profile.religion: Not publicly documented (no church membership or denomination found in public sources)
profile.background: VA House of Delegates, HD-79 (Richmond); attorney (J.D. Howard University; B.A. William & Mary), career in settlement administration. First elected Nov 2023, took office Jan 2024; re-elected Nov 2025. Committees: Courts of Justice, Education, Public Safety. VPAP caucus unity 94% (2025) / 96% (2026).

rae-cousins.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-79 (Richmond), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record; VPAP party-unity 94-96% with zero against-caucus votes on any social/culture-war issue. Life: ReproRising 2025 = A+ 'Champion'; co-patron HJ1 (2026) reproductive-freedom amendment; self-describes as a 'staunch advocate for the fundamental right to an abortion'; PP-Advocates endorsed (iVoterGuide). Marriage: same-sex-marriage amendment passed the House on party lines (HJ9 2025 58-35, full Democratic caucus in favor / HJ3 2026 67-31); as a 96%-unity Democrat with no social deviation she was in that majority. Family: explicitly 'rejects school choice policies and voucher schemes.' 2A: patron of HB217 (2026) banning 'assault firearms' and large-capacity magazines; Giffords-PAC endorsed. Co-patron HJ2 (2026) right-to-vote/felon-restoration amendment (recorded, not mapped to ballot-mechanics cells). Christian liberty, federal overreach, and immigration: 'did not answer' / no record — left null, not inferred from party. Religion not publicly documented.

0.note: ReproRising 2025 = A+ 'Champion'; co-patron of HJ1 (2026) reproductive-freedom amendment — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Self-describes as a 'staunch advocate for the fundamental right to an abortion'; voted the pro-abortion-access slate — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia.

0.note: Same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment passed the House on party lines (HJ9 2025 58-35, full Democratic caucus in favor); Cousins (96% caucus unity, zero social-issue deviations) was in that majority.

1.note: Voted with the unanimous Democratic caucus to enshrine same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ9 2025 / HJ3 2026).

0.note: Explicitly 'rejects school choice policies and voucher schemes, which divert tax dollars away from public schools.'

1.note: Patron of HB217 (2026) banning 'assault firearms' and large-capacity magazines; Giffords-PAC endorsed — opposite of the 2A position on bans/magazine limits.

profile.background: VA House of Delegates, HD-80 (part of Henrico). Former Communications Director, Virginia AFL-CIO. B.A. University of Richmond; M.A. George Washington Univ. First elected Nov 2023, took office Jan 2024; re-elected Nov 2025. Committees: Education; Labor & Commerce; Privileges & Elections. VPAP caucus unity 97% (2025) / 96% (2026).

destiny-levere-bolling.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-80 (Henrico), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Progressive HARD record; VPAP party-unity 96-97% with no conservative-direction deviation (off-caucus votes are procedural/local — speed cameras, alcohol delivery, paid-sick-leave mechanics). Life: ReproRising 2025 = A+ 'Champion'; co-patron of the reproductive-freedom amendment; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia (2023). Marriage: same-sex-marriage amendment passed the House on party lines (HJR9 2025 58-35, full Democratic caucus in favor / HJ3 2026); as a 96-97%-unity Democrat she was in that majority. Election: 'waved a green flag in support of HJ2' (voting-rights amendment) Jan 2025 — recorded, but not mapped to the ballot-mechanics election-integrity cells. 2A: only a SOFT progressive-guide 'gun violence prevention' framing with no specific bill and no individual Giffords grade (Giffords scores states, not delegates) — left null, not inferred. Family/child, Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration, public justice: no isolated roll-call — left null. Baptist (biographical).

0.note: ReproRising 2025 = A+ 'Champion'; co-patron/supporter of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: ReproRising A+ reflects voting the full pro-abortion-access slate — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia (2023).

0.note: Same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment passed the House on party lines (HJR9 2025 58-35, full Democratic caucus in favor); Bolling (96-97% unity, no social deviation) was in that majority.

1.note: Voted with the unanimous Democratic caucus to enshrine same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

profile.religion: Baptist; Associate Minister, New Bridge Baptist Church (per official VA House DOME record)
profile.background: VA House of Delegates, HD-81 (Charles City; parts of Chesterfield & Henrico). In office since Jan 2009. Committees: Appropriations, Education, Transportation. Former Richmond City Council (1999-2009) and School Board (1992-96). VPAP caucus unity 100% (2025) / 98% (2024).

delores-mcquinn.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-81 (Richmond area), sitting since 2009; Baptist associate minister at New Bridge Baptist Church. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] DEVIATION CHECK (her pastoral background): NONE — despite being an ordained Baptist minister she is a named co-patron of BOTH the abortion-rights amendment (HJ1) and the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ3/HJ9), and earned an A+ from ReproRising; no pro-life or pro-traditional-marriage break from her caucus. Life: co-patron HJ1 reproductive-freedom amendment; ReproRising 2025 = A+ 'Champion.' Marriage: named co-patron of HJ3 (2026)/HJ9 (2025) same-sex-marriage amendment. Economic: co-patron HB1 (2026) $15 minimum wage (recorded; does not map to the state economic_stewardship cells of CBDC/balanced-budget/usury/ESG, so left null). Election: co-patron HJ2 (2026) felon-voting-rights restoration (recorded, not mapped to ballot-mechanics cells). Only recent intra-party deviations are two 2024 public-safety/criminal-procedure bills (HB1583 firearm-threat penalties, HB2472 juvenile fingerprinting) — VPAP does not state direction, so not scored. 2A, family, Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration: no isolated directional roll-call — left null.

0.note: Named co-patron of HJ1 (reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment); ReproRising 2025 = A+ 'Champion' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: ReproRising A+ 'Champion' reflects the full pro-abortion-access voting record — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Named co-patron of HJ3 (2026)/HJ9 (2025), the constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's one-man-one-woman marriage provision.

1.note: Co-patron of the amendment enshrining a right to same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

profile.religion: Baptist (per official VA House member page)
profile.background: VA House of Delegates, HD-82 (City of Petersburg; parts of Dinwiddie, Prince George; Surry). CPA / state auditor; B.S. Accounting, Virginia Tech; MBA, Liberty University Online. First elected Nov 2025 (rematch win after losing by 53 votes in 2023); took office Jan 2026. Committees: Finance; Agriculture, Chesapeake & Natural Resources; Transportation. VPAP caucus unity 96% (2026).

kimberly-pope-adams.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-82 (Petersburg area), freshman sitting since Jan 2026; CPA/auditor, Baptist. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] STANDOUT MIXED RECORD with genuine documented cross-party credit. Contrary on social amendments: named co-patron of HJ1 (abortion-rights), HJ3 (same-sex marriage), and HJ2 (felon-voting restoration); endorsed by EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia. BUT documented PRO-2A deviation: despite a Giffords endorsement she voted NAY on three 2026 gun-control bills — HB1524 and its companion SB727 ('assault firearms' public-carry ban) and HB871 (mandatory safe-storage) — siding with House Republicans against her own caucus on all three (primary LIS roll-calls). Public justice: campaign platform explicitly supports 'sufficient state funding for Sheriff and Police departments' (pro-police-funding statement). Family (VEA endorsement), economic (labor endorsements): SOFT only, no mapped vote — left null. Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration: thin freshman record, no evidence — left null. HJ2 felon-restoration co-patronage recorded but not mapped to the ballot-mechanics election-integrity cells.

0.note: Named co-patron of HJ1 (2026) reproductive-freedom amendment; classified 'Pro-Choice' by Virginia Choice Tracker — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Co-patron of the reproductive-freedom amendment — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia.

0.note: Named co-patron of HJ3 (2026), the amendment repealing Virginia's one-man-one-woman marriage provision.

1.note: Co-patron of the amendment enshrining a right to same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

0.note: Campaign platform explicitly supports 'sufficient state funding for Sheriff and Police departments' — a public pro-law-enforcement statement (SOFT/platform, not a roll-call).

1.note: Voted NAY on HB1524 and its companion SB727 ('assault firearms' public-carry ban) and on HB871 (mandatory safe-storage) — three documented votes against gun-control restrictions, siding with Republicans against her own caucus.
